title: RNA extraction using the PureLink® RNA Mini Kit
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c48dd","text":"This Protocol details the extraction of total RNA using the PureLink® RNA Mini Kit.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"82n9","text":"Materials:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5b9vl","text":"Purelink RNA mini kit","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4r6cv","text":"70% EtOH in molecular biology grade water","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eok89","text":"Molecular biology grade water","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1q08m","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6dm3h","text":"Preparations:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cmvv9","text":"Wash buffer 2: add 60 ml of 100% EtOH directly to the bottle.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"454u7","text":"Lysis buffer: prepare a fresh aliquot containing 1% 2-mercaptoethanol (or 40 mM DTT).","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Remove the growth medium from the cells. We used a 15 cm 2 dish for each sample. The cells, collected in 1 ml of DPBS, were then divided, with ⅔ used for western blotting, and ⅓ used for the following protocol for RNA extraction.
Using RNase-free pipette tips, add the appropriate volume of lysis buffer prepared with 2-mercaptoethanol to sample.
Proceed with one of the following homogenization options at Room temperature :
Transfer the lysate to a homogenizer inserted in a collection tube and centrifuge at 12000 x g, °C, 2 minutes . Remove the homogenizer when done, or

Follow the steps below to bind, wash, and elute the RNA from your sample: Add one volume 70% ethanol to each volume of cell homogenate (prepare the sample as described in specific protocols. If part of the sample is lost during homogenization, adjust the volume of ethanol accordingly.
Vortex to mix thoroughly and to disperse any visible precipitate that may form after adding ethanol.
Transfer up to 700 µL of the sample (including any remaining precipitate) to the Spin Cartridge (with the Collection Tube).

Centrifuge at 12000 x g, °C, 15 seconds, Room temperature . Discard the flow-through, and reinsert the Spin Cartridge into the same collection tube. If you are processing the maximum starting amount of sample, you may centrifuge for up to 00:10:00 to completely pass the lysate through the Spin Cartridge.
Repeat Steps 6–7 until the entire sample is processed. Optional: If DNA-free total RNA is required, proceed to On-column PureLink® DNase Treatment Protocol.

Add 700 µL wash buffer I to the Spin Cartridge. Centrifuge at 12000 x g, °C, 15 seconds, Room temperature . Discard the flow-through and the Collection Tube. Place the Spin Cartridge into a new Collection Tube.
Add 500 µL Wash Buffer II with ethanol to the Spin Cartridge.
Centrifuge at 12000 x g, °C, 15 seconds, Room temperature . Discard the flow-through and reinsert the Spin Cartridge into the same collection tube.
Repeat Steps 10–11 once.
Centrifuge the Spin Cartridge at 12000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 - 00:02:00 to dry the membrane with attached the RNA. Discard the collection tube and insert the Spin Cartridge into a recovery tube.
Add 30 µL –3 × 100 µL RNase–Free Water to the center of the Spin Cartridge (see Elution Parameters). We diluted in 50 µL of water
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:01:00 .

Centrifuge the Spin Cartridge for 00:02:00 at ≥ 12000 x g, °C, , Room temperature to elute the RNA from the membrane into the recovery tube. If you are performing sequential elutions, collect all elutes into the same tube (see Elution Parameters).
Store your purified RNA or proceed to Analyzing RNA Yield and Quality or to DNase I Treatment after RNA purification.

Transfer the lysate to an appropriately sized RNase-free tube and homogenize using a rotorstator homogenizer at maximum speed for at least 00:00:45 . Centrifuge the homogenate at ∼ 2600 x g, °C, 5 minutes , then transfer the supernatant to a clean RNase-free tube.

Transfer the lysate to a 1.5 mL RNase–free tube and pass 5–10 times through an 18-21 gauge needle attached to an RNase-free syringe Centrifuge the homogenate at ∼ 2600 x g, °C, 5 minutes , then transfer the supernatant to a clean RNase-free tube We homogenized the cells by 10 passages in a 20G needle in 300 µL of lysis buffer
creator: Francesca Tonelli
